title: DEMO: automated App-review scanner is a committed demo build item
description: Open third-party publishing was chosen for the demo, so the E8 §5 LLM-as-judge App-review scanner (prompt-injection / data-exfiltration / jailbreak / scope-sanity over manifest+persona+skills) is NO LONGER deferred -- it gates every open publish and is the thing that makes open publishing safe. Real ML/eval build item. Run it OFF the DeepSeek inference GPU (sp-iui). Tune against curated red-team prompts + the seed apps. Affects E8 §5, E5 open-registry gate.
notes: REFRAMED via trust tiers (user decision): apps carry a per-version trust tier — unverified (published, not scanned) / scanned (passed this automated scanner) / reviewed (human). Runtime restrictions + user-facing warnings SCALE with tier; the enforced sandbox floor (egress + isolation + per-app consent) applies to ALL tiers. So the scanner is the 'scanned' tier mechanism, NOT a perfect single gate — a scanner miss is contained by the floor + the tighter unverified-tier sandbox + informed-consent disclosure. This de-risks the scanner's load-bearingness. Tier drops to 'scanned' on new version; permission escalation drops 'reviewed'. Seed apps ship 'reviewed'. Spec: E5 §5, E8 §5, scope §4.

title: DEMO-ROAST: threat-model inversion — user-steered agent IS the untrusted vector
description: The demo deferred egress enforcement (sp-ba5) and chose PLAIN CONTAINERS (no gVisor/microVM), both justified by 'no arbitrary creator code; Spawnery-vetted runtime only'. FALSE for the demo: the spawn runs a coding-style agent with shell/file/network tools, steered turn-by-turn by an untrusted free-tier stranger. The USER is the untrusted-code path. Consequences: open SSRF/egress-proxy/metadata-cred-theft by design (no egress allowlist), and container-escape/compute-abuse surface (plain containers + shell-capable agent). The security machinery cut for B+Y was load-bearing for B+Y too. FIX: treat the demo's spawn as hostile; pull egress floor (D2) + isolation hardening + resource limits (D3) + restricted toolset (D6) into the demo. Affects E1 isolation decision, E0/E8 egress, demo-mvp-scope.
notes: UN-DEFERRED: open third-party publishing makes hardened isolation mandatory IN the demo (creator-authored agents on others' data). No longer full-design-only.

title: DEMO-ROAST: ship a hard egress allowlist floor in the demo (not post-MVP)
description: Egress enforcement was deferred as 'permissions/consent' (sp-ba5), but the per-spawn EGRESS FLOOR (a hard default, not per-app consent UI) is cheap and closes most network abuse. FIX: per-spawn network policy blocking everything except the localhost model sidecar + GitHub API/host + any app-declared domains; explicitly DROP 169.254.169.254 (cloud metadata) + RFC1918 internal ranges. Owner = E1 (pod netns). This is the single highest value/cost fix in the demo. Closes SSRF, egress-proxy, metadata-cred-theft, arbitrary-host exfil.
notes: CONFIRMED demo-required: egress floor is mandatory for the open marketplace.

title: ROAST: privacy/brand claims dishonest as written — reframe
description: Found by 2/4 (security #2/#3/#7, product F1-F5/F7). Multiple headline claims are weasel-worded for the cloud/free path: (a) 'E2E / CP never sees content' — channel terminates at the NODE, which on cloud IS Spawnery and audits content; (b) 'BYO key never reaches us' — on cloud nodes the node decrypts the plaintext BYO key + audits content; (c) 'your data stays yours' — CP holds GitHub-App signer + can mint write tokens to your repo silently; (d) 'one-click' — see ROAST-6. Reframe all copy: 'encrypted in transit to the Spawnery-operated node that runs your agent; on cloud, content is audited for abuse.' Reserve absolute privacy claims for self-host only. Affects system-design §1/§6/§7/§9.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: CP is total SPOF + relay bandwidth cliff
description: Found by ops #1/#2. The single central CP relays ALL non-LAN-direct session ciphertext (every token, every turn) AND mints tokens AND schedules. A CP restart/deploy/OOM drops every active conversation simultaneously and blocks new sessions = 100% cloud blast radius. No HA/drain/reconnect story. Relaying all conversation bytes = 2x egress through one box, saturates on bandwidth + connection count long before compute. FIX: split a stateless horizontally-scaled relay dataplane from control logic; session-resumption tokens (reconnect without re-auth); blue/green relay; prioritize LAN-direct/BYO-ingress off the post-MVP shelf; publish SLO + client reconnect behavior. Affects E0 §1/§10, E1.

title: ROAST: BYO-on-cloud audit has no inspection point — mechanical gap
description: Found by 2/4 (arch #1, security #3). Four docs assert 'BYO-on-cloud is still audited' but NO component sees BYO model traffic: the sidecar calls the provider directly (E2 §1), the node only terminates the transport channel + forwards ACP frames over loopback (not model prompts/responses), and the E8 classifier is scoped to 'Spawnery-operated inference' which BYO bypasses. FIX: make the SIDECAR the single audit/classifier point (it's the only component seeing all model traffic, managed + BYO; it's a Spawnery-controlled container). Reconcile every 'node is the audit point' phrasing to the sidecar. Affects E2 §1/§4, E8 §1/§2, E0 §9/§10, system §3.

title: ROAST: home-machine singleton + no DR/failover
description: Found by ops #3/#4. (a) One physical box runs the whole free tier + DeepSeek on one GPU; realistic ceiling ~5-20 concurrent generations; if it dies the free tier is hard-down (burst triggers on OVERLOAD from a live heartbeat, not on a DEAD node). (b) No backup/DR for CP state (spawn index, catalog, GitHub App signing JWT, OAuth refresh tokens, vault wraps, audit store) — DB loss = users can't find spawns, must re-auth all storage, vault ciphertext gone. FIX: active/standby or dead-node->cloud failover (not just overload->burst); decouple inference GPU pool from container CPU pool; CP DB backup/PITR/geo-replication; GitHub App key + OAuth secrets in KMS not the DB; document what's reconstructable from spawn.yml. Affects system §1, E1 §5, E0.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: CP key-vending enables trivial MITM — 'E2E' unverifiable
description: Found by 3/4 reviewers (security #1/#2, ops #10, product F3). The client receives the node static pubkey FROM the CP with no out-of-band verification (deferred post-MVP). A malicious/compromised CP substitutes its own key, runs key agreement as the client, decrypts BYO keys + full conversation, re-encrypts to the real node. Classic unauthenticated-DH key substitution. Worse: Spawnery is simultaneously CA + relay + key-directory, so even cert pinning doesn't stop the operator minting a node cert. The 'CP relays only opaque ciphertext / never sees content' claim is structurally false against the operator. FIX: vend full CA-signed cert chain + pin Spawnery CA root in the client bundle (not fetched at runtime); make out-of-band/TOFU node-key pinning MVP (esp. self-host); add key-transparency log. Affects E0 §10, E4 §6/§8, E6 §2.

title: Cross-component APIs & contracts
description: The shared seams every component depends on. Defines: spawneryapp.yml manifest schema; spawn.yml schema (data-repo source of truth, secrets excluded); container orchestration interface (start/exec/stop); ACP client<->agent orchestration contract; storage-adapter interface (materialize/persist); model-sidecar OpenAI-compatible API + central-gateway protocol + metering/audit hooks; CP index API; edge-activator session-token/auth protocol; permission/consent + egress-allowlist schema; catalog/registry index API. Design-first; all other epics consume these contracts. See design doc sections 2,3,5,6,7,8,9,10.
notes:
Design doc (v1, interview complete): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-26-spawnery-e0-contracts-design.md — see Appendix decision log (E0.1–E0.9).
v2 corrections: CP = single central managed instance (self-host = node agents attaching to it); rendezvous relay in CP; s2s=gRPC; Apps agent-agnostic (agent chosen at spawn, manifest declares compat); skills imported via agent's native process; storage detail -> E3; personalization + permissions -> post-MVP (TODO.md). Amended system-design §1/§4/§10.

title: Runtime & orchestration core
description: Control plane, node agent, container lifecycle (ephemeral, scale-to-zero), ACP orchestration layer, pluggable isolation-backend abstraction (concrete impls deferred), scheduler/placement (local-first, audit-flag set at placement). Design doc sections 3,4.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-spawnery-e1-runtime-core-design.md. Decisions: per-agent base image + mount definition (no per-(App,agent) build); cold-start every wake (warm pools post-MVP); placement = open app + owner node -> owner node else cloud; 2-D burst (CPU->offload containers, GPU->offload inference); idle/close/cap teardown, resume from /data; pod = base[agent+bridge+toolset]+sidecar; Docker/Podman backend first. Refined E0 §7/§3.

title: DEMO-ROAST: GitHub-only storage inverts the target market
description: Demo storage = GitHub, but the pitch is NON-TECHNICAL users who don't have GitHub accounts and won't authorize an App grant. So the only people who can onboard are developers -- the segment LEAST impressed by a hosted wiki (they self-host). The demo is hardest for the intended user and least compelling for the reachable one. FIX: ship MANAGED storage (Spawnery-hosted, open-format, exportable) as the DEFAULT, with 'connect your own GitHub' as the power-user option (architecture already supports managed storage). Default any spawned/managed repo to PRIVATE (no public-repo abuse as free CDN). Affects E3, E6 onboarding, demo-mvp-scope, system §5.
notes: DECIDED: managed storage = DEFAULT (web-browsable + download/clone); Drive/iCloud/OneDrive (git bundle) = BYO-cloud option; GitHub = power-user. Spawned repos default private. Scope doc §4 updated.

title: DEMO-ROAST: demo is a weak experiment — doesn't test the thesis; no success metric
description: The business bet is differentiation (your-data + your-model + creator marketplace). The demo has no model choice (DeepSeek only), undercut data ownership (CP holds GitHub token, all audited), and ZERO marketplace (4 first-party apps, no creators). So a demo win only proves 'free hosted AI wiki is mildly appealing' (already suggested by NotebookLM/Mem/GPT-Store) and a loss is ambiguous (idea vs gutted execution). Also no defined success metric/audience/threshold => the experiment can't fail, so it can't inform go/no-go. And no retention hypothesis (why open the wiki on day 8?). FIX: define the explicit demo hypothesis + metric + audience before build; add a retention hook (e.g. weekly 'capture what you learned' nudge); be honest this validates one app's stickiness, not the platform.
notes:
ELABORATION (3 layers):
1) THESIS COVERAGE: tests ~1 of 5 load-bearing bets. H1 (wiki demand) weakly/confounded; H2 (data+model differentiation) NO; H3 (creators publish) NO; H4 (marketplace flywheel) NO; H5 (non-technical onboarding) anti-tested (GitHub gate filters them out).
2) CONFOUNDING: demo changes 5 vars at once vs ideal product (weak model, cold-start latency, GitHub wall, capped access, unrepresentative audience) => low retention is unattributable (idea? model? latency? onboarding? audience?). Unreadable signal.
3) NO METRIC/THRESHOLD/KILL-CRITERION: viral-tweet acquisition => will measure signups (vanity; measures tweet reach not product value). No pre-committed 'if X<Y by Z we do NOT build' => un-failable => can't inform go/no-go.
KEY RISK: the WIKI may retain WORST (PKM cold-start-value death spiral: only valuable once big, won't invest before valuable). The thing that breaks the spiral = proactive agent synthesis = the real differentiator = NOT in demo scope. So demo may ship the non-magical wiki and wrongly conclude 'wikis don't retain'. Irony: the coaches (cut by sp-jg3) have STRONGER built-in return loops (habit=daily, interview=event-driven) than the flagship.
META: demo conflates (a) technical proof [vertical slice does this, low risk] with (b) market validation [needs experiment design]. Don't read (a)'s usage as (b).
CONSTRUCTIVE: (i) name the single riskiest assumption (likely: does a personal LLM wiki retain a representative user past W2); (ii) test it CONCIERGE-style with 10-20 target non-technical users + high-quality model + manual glue BEFORE/around the build (~0 eng, removes confounders); (iii) pre-commit metric (activation + W2/W4 retention, NOT signups) + threshold + audience + decision; (iv) if building B+Y anyway, kill cheap confounders (managed storage sp-0a2, model-quality floor, proactive-synthesis if retention is what you measure).
UPDATE: open marketplace now IN demo scope -> the demo tests a slice of H3/H4 (do creators publish? do users spawn 3rd-party apps?). Improves thesis coverage from ~1/5 to ~2.5/5. STILL UNSOLVED: no success metric/threshold/representative-audience, and the wiki PKM cold-start-value retention risk. Define the experiment before build.

title: DEMO-ROAST: pod resource limits + per-user quotas + isolation hardening
description: Single GPU box + plain containers + free tier = compute-abuse + free-inference-farming + DoS. One abuser can exhaust the GPU and deny the whole free tier (capacity ceiling = attack surface). FIX: cgroup CPU/mem/disk/pids limits on every pod (stop mining/fork-bombs/disk-fill); per-user concurrent-spawn cap; per-user daily token/inference quota; reconsider gVisor for the demo (cheap-ish, given the agent runs untrusted-user-steered code). Sybil friction: prefer GitHub-auth (more friction/age signal than throwaway Gmail) or a phone/age gate before free inference. Affects E1, E8 §3 free-tier signals.

title: ROAST: GitHub polling + per-turn persist = rate limits & I/O cliff
description: Found by ops #6/#7. (a) CP polls GitHub for latest tag->SHA at EVERY createSpawn + auto-resume: adds a sync round-trip, ~5000/hr installation rate-limit shared with provisioning+clone+push, and a hard dependency (GitHub down => no new auto spawns). (b) Persist per turn = git push every turn (rate-limit pressure) and for blob, 'git bundle --all' + FULL re-upload of entire history every turn => latency/bandwidth grow linearly with repo size (100MB+ per turn for an old spawn). FIX: cache resolved tag->SHA w/ TTL (periodic poll populates it; createSpawn never blocks on GitHub; fallback to last-known-good when GitHub down); debounce/coalesce persistence + background push; incremental blob bundles (since last ref) or pull folder-as-git-remote into MVP for blob. Affects E5 §2, E3 §2/§5.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: consent/egress enforcement deferred but presented as shipped; no owner
description: Found by product F14, arch #12. system §9 presents 'user consents to scopes + runtime enforces egress allowlist' as present-tense architecture, but E0 §9/§11 + E8 mark permissions/consent/egress ENFORCEMENT post-MVP (E8 is the stopgap). Also no epic OWNS egress enforcement when it lands, and BYO-direct inference means the allowlist is per-spawn-dynamic (depends on the BYO baseUrl), not static 'provider+storage only'. FIX: align system §9 to post-MVP; assign egress-enforcement ownership to E1 (pod netns layer), configured from the manifest permission block + the spawn's model baseUrl; note the dynamic-allowlist complication. Affects system §9, E0 §9, E1.
notes: REOPENED + UN-DEFERRED: open third-party marketplace requires the manifest permissions block + spawn-time consent + egress ENFORCEMENT in the demo. Was incorrectly deferred when scope was first-party-only.

title: ROAST: stale-reference reconciliation sweep
description: Found by arch #2/#3. Superseded decisions never propagated: (a) system-design §4 diagram + §10 still say 'TLS terminates at the bridge / client pins bridge cert' — contradicts the node-terminates E2E model (E0 §10/E6.1); (b) E0 §7 still says the bridge 'exposes ACP over an authenticated WebSocket (TLS terminates in the container)' — contradicts E0's own §10; (c) E2 decision-log E2.2 still says 'sealed to the node pubkey, node unseals' — superseded by ride-the-session-channel; (d) system §7 + decision-log #13 still say 'client hands plaintext key directly to the sidecar / direct-in-container' — now goes client->node->inject. FIX: sweep all 'TLS at bridge', 'bridge cert', 'direct-in-container', 'seal-to-node' references to the unified per-session E2E channel (node terminates, forwards over loopback). Affects system §4/§7/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: open-core 'single-box docker-compose' claim is false; portability oversold
description: Found by arch #6, product F12. system §1 still says self-host 'runs identically as a single-box docker compose stack' but E0.10 made the CP a single central managed instance: a self-hosted node is INERT without the CP (can't enroll, get session tokens, resolve apps, route managed inference; the only client is the CP-served SPA). Also 'carry your repo to ANY Spawnery and re-spawn identically' — there is only ONE CP (self-hosted CP post-MVP), and spawn.yml references a CREATOR's definition repo you don't control (delist/delete => can't reconstruct). FIX: rewrite system §1 open-core constraint (self-host = node attaching to central CP; standalone post-MVP); split portability claim into 'content fully portable (plain files)' vs 'spawn config runs on Spawnery'; let users snapshot the App definition into their own repo so a delisted app still re-spawns. Affects system §1/§2/§11/§12.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: storage-token custody undercuts 'your data is yours'
description: Found by arch #9, product F1. CP holds the GitHub App signer + OAuth refresh tokens and can mint read/write tokens to the user's repo at will, silently, server-side, no per-action consent, no user-visible log. The E2E machinery protects the model KEY but not the DATA (the bigger asset). FIX: reconcile the top-level claim (narrow to 'CP never sees conversation content; storage access is CP-mediated via scoped tokens in MVP'); ship a user-visible storage-access log (every token mint + push, with reason) in MVP; surface CP storage access in E8 transparency UI; pull user-held/E2E-sealed storage creds forward if feasible. Affects E3 §3, E8 §6, system §1/§5.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: 'one-click' is a lie — onboarding gauntlet for non-technical user
description: Found by product F7/F8/F13. First-run for the target user: OAuth + invent a vault passphrase + store a 24-word BIP-39 mnemonic + approve a GitHub per-repo grant + a 4-field wizard (App version / choose agent / choose model / choose storage) + cold-start wait. ~6-7 decision points, two of them (passphrase, seed phrase) alien concepts, for a user who wanted 'make a wiki'. 'Choose agent' is meaningless to them. FIX: genuine one-click default (managed storage + managed model + recommended agent); LAZY vault (no passphrase until first BYO key — free tier never needs it); hide agent choice behind Advanced; if a passphrase is set, make recovery codes mandatory not opt-in. Affects E6 §3, E4 §1/§3/§5, system §1.
notes: Largely RESOLVED by demo cut: no vault/BYOK -> onboarding = OAuth + connect GitHub + spawn.
close_reason: Resolved by demo-MVP scope (no vault/BYOK; onboarding = OAuth+GitHub+spawn)

title: ROAST: coding-agent-as-coach is unvalidated and load-bearing
description: Found by product F10 (arch #5). E7 §6 itself flags that a coding-style agent is tuned to 'do a file task,' not conversational coaching — then punts. 3 of 4 launch apps (habit/goal worst, sysdesign best, wiki fine) coerce a task-executor into a conversationalist via persona alone. A habit coach that answers 'I'm struggling' by grepping checkins/ and committing a file is uncanny — and it's the app that defines the emotional brand. FIX: dogfood the habit coach with real non-technical users for ~2 weeks; measure 'felt like a coach vs a robot'; be ready to drop habit coach from launch. Don't ship the weakest-fit app to prove generality. Affects E7 §1/§4/§6.
notes: Doc gate strengthened in E7 §6 (dogfood habit coach ~2wk + free-DeepSeek compat check). Issue stays OPEN as real pre-launch validation work, not closable by docs.

title: ROAST: vault hardening — recovery codes, KDF, Argon2 params
description: Found by security #4/#5 (product F13). (a) Multi-use recovery codes stored as wrap_rc at the CP = standing, non-expiring, undetectable skeleton key; CP compromise + the code = all BYO secrets forever, silently. (b) KDF(RC) is unspecified (only the passphrase path specifies Argon2id). (c) Argon2id 64MiB/t=3 tuned for browser perf is weak for operator-held ciphertext; no passphrase strength policy anywhere. (d) Recovery codes opt-in => non-technical user silently bricks vault. FIX: single-use recovery codes (invalidate wrap_rc on use); specify KDF(RC) (Argon2id or HKDF w/ domain separation); enforce passphrase strength floor + raise memory cost w/ progress UI; notify on any wrap_rc fetch; consider offline-only recovery (never upload wrap_rc). Affects E4 §3/§3a/§4/§8.

title: Launch coach repos
description: Definition repos + bundled tools for the launch lineup: zork (no storage; first vertical-slice smoke test), llm-wiki (flagship/wedge), habit/goal coach, system-design-interview coach. Design doc sections 11,12.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e7-launch-coaches-design.md. Decisions: one existing ACP coding-style agent in MVP base (concrete pick = build-time spike w/ acceptance criteria); zork = minimal data repo + save file (pulls E3 into the slice; storage-less smoke test allowed first); wiki = Markdown files in /data + file-nav/full-text retrieval; coaches = cross-session memory as human-readable files in /data reloaded per session; all 4 are 'files in /data' apps differing only by persona/skills/schema. Flagged interaction-pattern risk (coding agent doing conversational coaching) for the agent spike + roasting.

title: App packaging & catalog
description: spawneryapp.yml spec + validation; definition-repo format; versioning (git tags, auto-upgrade + opt-out pin + escalation/snapshot/changelog guardrails); open registry; private-App human-review pipeline; catalog index + marketplace backend. Design doc sections 2,8.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e5-packaging-catalog-design.md. Decisions: MVP poll model (no webhook; CP polls latest tag at createSpawn -> SHA, dispatches SHA to node); hybrid registration (formal optional for catalog listing + ad-hoc URL spawn for public repos; private requires registration); CP-side catalog index w/ basic browse+search; open = automated checks instant list; private = automated + human review queue (review content owned by E8); versioning via immutable SHAs w/ auto-upgrade + pin + pre-upgrade snapshot; apiVersion spawnery/v1.

title: Web client
description: Catalog browse; spawn wizard (connect storage, pick model, personalization, consent); chat UI over the direct ACP channel; spawn management + portability (export / re-spawn from data repo). Design doc sections 8,10.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-spawnery-e6-web-client-design.md. Key decision: per-session E2E channel (node-static + client-ephemeral key agreement) encrypts ALL non-metadata (convos + secrets), CP relays opaque, node terminates+forwards over loopback; metadata plaintext. SPA no BFF; WebCrypto; ephemeral client key; responsive web/PWA MVP. Superseded E0 §10 (bridge-cert) and E2 §2 (per-secret seal); reconciled E0/E1/E2.

title: Model layer
description: Per-spawn model-gateway sidecar (OpenAI-compatible, LiteLLM-style); cloud central gateway (managed keys, route to local DeepSeek/burst, metering); BYO direct-to-provider; metering pipeline; audit hook point. Design doc section 6.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-spawnery-e2-model-layer-design.md. Decisions: translation/routing split (sidecar=BYO direct, gateway=managed); BYO key sealed to NODE pubkey, CP-relayed opaque, node unseals+injects (CP never sees plaintext; node autogen keypair, pubkey enrolled, CP vends); managed keys central-only; local DeepSeek behind OpenAI-compat server; route local/cloud per GPU-burst; gateway meters + enforces free-tier caps; MVP managed DeepSeek + BYO OpenAI-compat.

title: Identity & secrets
description: OAuth login (Google + GitHub, GitHub doubles as storage connection); account model + creator role; server-blind vault passphrase; client-side BYO-key encryption + end-to-end delivery to the sidecar. Design doc section 7.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e4-identity-secrets-design.md. Decisions: Argon2id KDF + BIP-39 24-word multi-use opt-in recovery codes (envelope wrap, CP server-blind); node enrollment via one-time owner-scoped token -> node-generated keypair -> CA-signed mTLS cert binding node to owner (pubkey vended to clients); vault unlock once per browser session, VK in-memory only, inactivity timeout. Asserted: OAuth Google+GitHub PKCE (CP as auth server; GitHub App double-duty login+storage); single user + creator role; CP-signed JWT session tokens verified offline by nodes.

title: Storage layer
description: Uniform /data git-working-tree mount; provider-adapter framework; GitHub App adapter (fine-grained per-repo); blob adapter (git bundle); single-writer/conflict handling; spawn.yml read/write; optional conversation-history persistence. Design doc sections 2,5.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e3-storage-design.md. Decisions: CP-managed short-lived storage tokens, refresh-on-demand via gRPC; CP provisions destination + node clones/scaffolds; layout = .spawnery/ for internals, App data at repo root; GitHub-native first then blob (git bundle); per-turn persist + idle/exit autosave; non-fast-forward = LWW surfaced; pre-upgrade snapshot via git tag. Added storage block back to E0 §3 under E3 authority.

title: DEMO-ROAST: restrict agent toolset + audit-store liability + drop coaches
description: Three cheap demo de-riskers: (a) RESTRICT the agent's toolset for launch apps to file/search over /data (NO arbitrary shell/network fetch) -- the wiki doesn't need a shell, and the shell is the abuse vector; matches the original 'vetted tools' intent and shrinks the attack surface enormously. (b) AUDIT-STORE LIABILITY: do NOT persist severe-category flagged content (CSAM etc.) to the 30d audit store -- log the verdict + block, drop the bytes; storing it creates the liability it was meant to reduce. (c) SHIP WIKI-ONLY (+ zork as a toy), DROP both coaches from the demo -- weak-local-model-sensitive, uncanny, sp-pgw-unvalidated; fewer apps = less surface + best foot forward. Affects E7, E8 §1, demo-mvp-scope.

title: ROAST: ops hardening grab-bag
description: Found by ops #8/#9/#12/#13, product F6/F11. (a) Inline classifier runs per-turn on the SAME scarce home GPU (could ~2x load) — move to cheap CPU/small model, async fast-path for non-severe. (b) Burst gap/thundering herd: provisioning from zero takes 30s-min while home is already saturated — keep 1 pre-warmed standby, admission control + 'warming up' state, scale on leading indicator. (c) Audit store growth: full-content per turn — size/cost it, alert on stuck purge, KMS-managed key, tamper-evident access log. (d) Ad-hoc URL spawn: rate-limit per user, github.com-only, confirm scanner gates before exec (SSRF-ish). (e) Token refresh storms: proactive refresh + jittered expiry + coalesce. (f) Model contract may filter the free DeepSeek OUT of coaches needing big context (F6) — verify all 4 launch apps run acceptably on free DeepSeek or it's a bait-and-switch. Affects E8, E1 §5, E5 §3, E3 §3, E2.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: protocol/contract drift across E0/E1/E3/E4
description: Found by arch #8/#10/#11, security #6/#8/#9, ops #10/#11. Grouped contract cleanups: (a) createSpawn (E3 §4) vs startSpawn (E1 §3) — two verbs for node-side bring-up, only startSpawn in the E0 §6 contract; define both (provision vs wake) or unify. (b) refreshStorageToken (E3) missing from the E0 §6 node->CP verb list. (c) POST /spawns ownership: E0 §5a says CP 'initializes repo + writes spawn.yml' but E3 §4 says the NODE scaffolds/writes/pushes after placement — define sync vs async + who writes spawn.yml. (d) startSpawn payload carries sessionTokenPubkey but E4 §7 distributes the CP signing pubkey at enrollment — drop the redundant field. (e) Session token not channel-bound (bind to handshake transcript + add jti/replay cache + node verifies its own nodeId). (f) Enrollment token theft window — device-code style confirm + show node fingerprint to owner. (g) Separate node mTLS auth key from the E2E key-agreement static key; define rotation/revocation. Affects E0 §5a/§6, E1 §3, E3 §4, E4 §6/§7.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: cold-start-every-wake = bad UX for coaches; no latency budget
description: Found by 3/4 (arch #7, product F9, ops #5). Realistic first-token on resume = 5-15s warm-image/small-repo, 30s-2min+ cold-image/large-repo/busy-GPU (image pull + pod create + git clone /data + agent boot + skill load + key agreement + model first token). Coaches' whole value is continuity, but every 'checking in' pays full cold-start + full-thread reload — and the loyal daily user with a big thread gets the WORST latency + re-billed replay tokens. No doc owns this number. FIX: pull warm-pool/keep-alive + node repo-cache (resume=fetch not clone) + pre-pulled images + incremental/summarized thread load into MVP for coaching apps; loading-state UX in E6; set a first-token-on-resume acceptance metric. Affects E1 §3/§4, E6 §3, E7.
notes: RESOLVED (user): accept cold-start startup times for MVP; warm-pool/repo-cache/incremental-load DECLINED. Key correction: cold start = NEW session, NO conversation-thread replay. Continuity = agent reads structured files in /data at session start (not transcript-into-context). Kills the 'loyal user worst latency' + 're-bill replay' concerns. Tune idle timeout to match LLM provider prompt-cache TTL (~5min) since cache expires regardless. DOC FIX NEEDED: E1 §3 / E7 §4 / E6 §3 currently say resume 'reloads the prior thread to restore context' -- correct to 'fresh session; continuity via file reads in /data'.
close_reason: Resolved: accept cold-start times; continuity = file reads not thread replay (demo-mvp-scope §4)

title: Trust, safety & audit
description: Permission/consent enforcement; egress allowlist enforcement; audit pipeline (sidecar conversation-content logging for Spawnery-operated cloud only); abuse signals; flagging/takedown for the open registry. Design doc section 9.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e8-trust-safety-audit-design.md. Decisions: audit = full content on Spawnery-operated infra, encrypted, ~30d TTL auto-purge, break-glass access, disclosed (self-host exempt, BYO-on-cloud audited); review = scanner-first (LLM-as-judge over manifest+persona+skills) human-on-flag; enforcement = tiered (live block severe-only + async delist/suspend leaving user data untouched + appeal); inline classifier shares infra with review scanner (real ML build item); free-tier content-blind abuse signals. E8 is the MVP safety net standing in for deferred permission/egress enforcement.

title: Cloud burst
description: DEFERRED (post-MVP). Autoscaler/provisioner; cloud node agents; burst inference + container routing; capacity signaling from the home machine. Design doc sections 4,12 (sub-project 9).

title: Billing, payments & rev-share
description: DEFERRED (post-MVP). Metering->billing; subscription tiers; BYOK tier; premium-models / private-apps gating; creator payouts and marketplace rev-share. Design doc sections 12,13 (sub-project 10).
